changes_made: Changed k8s_apiserver_audit_log_backup_configured to k8s_audit_log_backup_configured for consistency with resource naming
review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with the CCCS CP-2 control, focusing on backup and recovery aspects critical to contingency planning. However, a minor naming adjustment was made for consistency with the resource naming conventions.
naming_improvements: Use 'audit' instead of 'apiserver' for audit log related functions to align with resource naming standards
title: CCCS CP-2: Contingency Plan
step1_reasoning: The CCCS CP-2: Contingency Plan control focuses on ensuring that contingency plans are in place for system recovery and continuity. In a Kubernetes context, this translates to ensuring that critical components such as etcd have backup configurations, audit logs are backed up, disaster recovery policies for pods and workloads are defined, and overall cluster backup and restore procedures are documented. These checks ensure that the Kubernetes environment can be restored and maintained in the event of a failure, aligning with the contingency plan requirements.
